name: Gardens of la Alfranca and Carrasco Pond — circular through the Pinares de la Alfranca
name_original: Jardines de la Alfranca y Balsa del Carrasco — circular por los Pinares de la Alfranca
region_intro: Hiking around Pastriz, Zaragoza, offers routes primarily characterized by the Ebro River and its surrounding natural areas. The landscape features riverine paths, pine forests (Pinares de la Alfranca), and agricultural lands. Elevation changes are generally minimal, making many trails accessible. Best hiking trails around Pastriz The most popular hiking route is Gardens of la Alfranca and Carrasco Pond — circular through the Pinares de la Alfranca, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 44 minutes to complete. This easy circular route explores the pine forests and gardens of La Alfranca. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ebro Natural Trail Loop from El Burgo de Ebro, a moderate 8.6 miles (13.9 km) path. This trail follows the Ebro Natural Trail, offering views of the river and its surroundings. Local hikers also love the Ebro River – Valdegurriana Locks loop from Cartuja Baja, a 11.9 miles (19.2 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 54 minutes. Hiking around Pastriz is defined by the Ebro River, pine forests, and open agricultural areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more moderate excursions. The routes in Pastriz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 300 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Pastriz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there around Pastriz?
A: Pastriz offers over 150 hiking and walking routes. The area is known for its diverse paths along the Ebro River, through pine forests, and across agricultural lands, providing options for various preferences and skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Pastriz?
A: The best time to hike in Pastriz is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, ideal for exploring the riverine landscapes and pine forests. Summers can be very hot, and winters, while generally mild, might be less appealing for longer excursions.

Q: Are there easy hikes suitable for beginners or families in Pastriz?
A: Yes, Pastriz has many easy routes perfect for beginners and families. Approximately 60 of the trails are rated as easy. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974939209/gardens-of-la-alfranca-and-carrasco-pond-circular-through-the-pinares-de-la-alfranca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gardens of la Alfranca and Carrasco Pond — circular through the Pinares de la Alfranca&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.3-mile (6.9 km) circular route that explores the beautiful pine forests and gardens.

Q: Are there any circular walks available in Pastriz?
A: Many of the trails around Pastriz are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980025102/ebro-natural-trail-loop-from-el-burgo-de-ebro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ebro Natural Trail Loop from El Burgo de Ebro&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.6-mile (13.9 km) loop following the Ebro River, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14952682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock Garden – Galachos de La Alfranca loop from La Puebla de Alfindén&lt;/a&gt; is another moderate circular option.

Q: What options are there for longer or more challenging hikes?
A: While most routes have minimal elevation changes, there are longer, moderate options for those seeking more distance. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17550968" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ebro River – Valdegurriana Locks loop from Cartuja Baja&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11.9-mile (19.2 km) trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31902927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ebro River – Soto de Cantalobos loop from Cartuja baja&lt;/a&gt; is a similar 12.2-mile (19.6 km) moderate route, both offering extensive riverine exploration.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around Pastriz?
A: Many trails in the Pastriz area, especially those along the Ebro River and through the Pinares de la Alfranca, are generally dog-friendly. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural lands, and to check specific trail regulations before you go.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting along the trails?
A: Yes, the riverine environments and pine forests around Pastriz offer good opportunities for wildlife spotting. The Galachos de La Alfranca, for instance, is a protected natural area known for its birdlife. You might spot various bird species, especially near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334206" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsa del Carrasco Bird Observatory&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5257925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsa del Cascarro Observatory&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or places to explore near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, there are several interesting landmarks. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1355723" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdegurriana Locks&lt;/a&gt; along the Ebro River, which are part of the river's historical infrastructure. For cultural exploration, the nearby city of Zaragoza offers significant sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3007697" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basilica of Our Lady of the Pillar&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4531361" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral of the Savior (La Seo of Zaragoza)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find any natural attractions or lakes along the routes?
A: The region features several natural attractions, particularly related to water. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5335575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salada de Mediana&lt;/a&gt; is a salt flat, offering a unique landscape. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1473905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pina de Ebro Dam&lt;/a&gt;, which is a significant man-made structure influencing the local water bodies.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Pastriz?
A: The komoot community highly rates hiking in Pastriz, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers often praise the peaceful riverine paths, the accessibility of the trails with minimal elevation, and the natural beauty of the Pinares de la Alfranca.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads?
A: Yes, parking is generally available at or near the starting points of many popular trails, especially in areas like La Alfranca or near the villages along the Ebro River. It's advisable to check specific route details on komoot for precise parking information.

Q: How can I reach the hiking trails using public transport?
A: While some trailheads might be accessible by local bus services connecting Pastriz to Zaragoza and surrounding villages, public transport options can be limited for reaching more remote starting points. Checking local bus schedules for routes connecting to areas like La Alfranca or El Burgo de Ebro is recommended.
tips: Small bird observatory on the Carrasco raft where, depending on the season visited, we can observe different types of birds. We will access the raft through some narrow walkways.

Natural space dependent on the government of Aragon with several interpretation centers, gardens and labyrinths of hedges and stones that the little ones will have a great time. Possibility to have a picnic.
